questions.description: John worked for VultureWings DAC, a low cost airline, as an aircraft… Advise VultureWings on any potential tort…
questions.text: John worked for VultureWings DAC, a low cost airline, as an aircraft maintenance specialist. John was working on a VultureWings aircraft as it was at the gate preparing to load passengers for an outgoing flight. John found himself working on the hydraulic system of the wing. It was a difficult job particularly as VultureWings did not have the necessary equipment that John could work on it from the ground. Although the maintenance workers had all been trained to not work on any aircraft without the proper equipment, John was conscious this would be the fourth plane which was delayed due to maintenance issues. As a result he decided to climb onto the wing to undertake the repairs. Jennifer, a passenger on that flight, had arrived early and was sitting at the gate. She could see John working on the aircraft and found it hugely interesting. At one point, she caught John's eye and waved at him. John looked up and waved back. In doing this he lost his balance and found himself sliding off the wing unable to stop his fall. Jennifer could not bring herself to look and could only imagine the terrible consequences of John's fall, certain that he was dead. In fact John was tethered to the wing and so never fell to the ground and was in fact unhurt. But Jennifer could not shake the image from her mind and now finds herself with a debilitating fear of flying. Advise VultureWings on any potential tort liability to Jennifer that might arise in this situation.

questions.description: In tort law the limitation periods within which an action must be taken have been so amended that far from providing…
questions.text: In tort law the limitation periods within which an action must be taken have been so amended that far from providing for certainty in litigation, limitation periods have now become a source of increased uncertainty. Critically discuss this statement in light of the current rules on limitation of actions in the law of tort

questions.description: Eamonn has become increasingly worried about the changing environment and,… Advise Eamonn as to whether he has any…
questions.text: Eamonn has become increasingly worried about the changing environment and, in particular, the potential for global warming which could lead to life threatening water shortages in the country. With that in mind, he has decided to make certain changes on his property that will assist his neighbours in the event of any catastrophic environmental disaster. Eamonn has installed a rain water harvesting system all around his house and sheds. With this system, all the rain that falls, and which would normally just be allowed run off the rooves of the house and sheds, is gathered together in a single large water treatment container where it is purified so that it can be safely drunk by humans. A series of pipes then channels the water to underground storage tanks. There is an inbuilt safety mechanism which means that if the storage tanks become too full, a controlled amount of drinking water is released from the tanks so as to avoid any risk of flooding any of the surrounding neighbours if the water is released too quickly. The system has worked exceptionally well but in recent days, the level of rain experienced has exceeded all known records by a huge amount, and far beyond what the system is designed to deal with. Accordingly, the tanks release an uncontrolled amount of water in the surrounding neighbourhood, causing considerable flooding. Eamonn is convinced that he is not to blame, as he only collected the rain which fell naturally and so the flooding is not his fault. Advise Eamonn as to whether he has any potential liability in tort law.

questions.description: Critically examine the defence of consent or waiver under s.34(1)(b) of…
questions.text: Critically examine the defence of consent or waiver under s.34(1)(b) of the Civil Liability Act, 1961

questions.description: Nicola is famous on a video sharing website where she regularly reviews…
questions.text: Nicola is famous on a video sharing website where she regularly reviews products that she buys. The reviews include demonstrating how the product is used, special tricks that can be used to further enhance the utility of the item and her overall rating of the item in terms of value for money, reliability etc. Nicola has become something of a celebrity on the website with over 100,000 followers. As a result, she has been noticed by major companies who now specifically send her free products to test and trial on her video, hoping to use her reviews to profile their products to the general public, particularly the younger demographic who use these channels. The companies do not pay her for this, but she does get to keep all the products she receives, whether or not she reviews them. A while back, Nicola received a microwave from Siny Electronics to review. Eventually in the last few days she decided that she would review the item. Under pressure of time, Nicola did not bother to read the instruction booklet and presumed that it looked like a microwave that she had previously reviewed. As a result, on her video she said that the great thing about it was that you could reheat metal takeaway containers directly in the microwave. In fact this was wrong. However, Carmel did as Nicola suggested causing her main house fuse to blow and necessitating expensive electrical repairs to her house. Carmel seeks your advice as to whether Siny Electronics have any liability in the law of tort for Nicola's review.

questions.description: Critically discuss whether the doctrine of res ipsa locquitur in Irish tort law should be abolished or not.
questions.text: Critically discuss whether the doctrine of res ipsa locquitur in Irish tort law should be abolished or not.

questions.description: Bill had been friendly with Donal, but over time the relationship had…
questions.text: Bill had been friendly with Donal, but over time the relationship had soured for no obvious reason. Donal was upset and one evening, he called into Bill's house as he wanted to clear the air between them. Bill said that he would make Donal coffee. But Donal said he had changed his mind and was going home. Bill then went to the front door and locked it, saying "No one leaves until we have had our coffee." Bill went to the kitchen and came back with the coffee. He gave one to Donal saying "I made this one especially for you. Be sure to drink it all." Donal replied that it was getting late and he wanted to go home but Bill said no one was leaving until they had drank their coffee. When Donal finished his coffee he stood up to leave. Bill stood up as well and said "That coffee was poisoned. You will be dead within 24 hours, so you are now free to go. There is no cure. Goodbye!" Donal fled the house and drove straight to the hospital where his stomach was pumped but in fact the coffee had not been poisoned. Advise Donal as to any potential claims in tort [only] that he might have against Bill in these circumstances

questions.description: John purchased the latest electric vehicle, an SUV which operated… Advise Mary as to who is the cause of her broken leg…
questions.text: John purchased the latest electric vehicle, an SUV which operated exclusively on battery power. However, he was continually disappointed with the range of the vehicle as it seldom travelled more than 50% of the distance he had been promised when buying it. As a result he took it back to the dealer who said that they would run some tests on it to see what the issue was. As it was undergoing testing, Sam, a mechanic at the dealership thought that he could smell burning but could not see any smoke or flame so dismissed it. He then connected the vehicle to a supercharging point and began to pump in more charge into the battery. No matter how long it was connected to the charger the battery never appeared to get fully charged. Sam took the vehicle out for a test drive. On that drive the car burst into flames. Sam jumped from the vehicle but had made no attempt to put on the handbrake. As a result the vehicle rolled down an incline still on fire. Some of the sparks from the vehicle landed close to the pram that Mary had her baby in. Mary, seeing the sparks, grabbed the baby from the pram and attempted to run as far away as she could but tripped over a piece of broken pavement about 10 metres from the pram, breaking her leg. On examination, it appears that John had watched an online video which showed how to extend the range of an electric vehicle by keeping the battery pack warmer through a small heating device which he had attached to the car. Advise Mary as to who is the cause of her broken leg under the law of tort.

questions.description: Critically discuss the law with regard to establishing that a duty of care exists in tort actions, paying particular…
questions.text: Critically discuss the law with regard to establishing that a duty of care exists in tort actions, paying particular attention to the development from Donoghue v Stevenson to date.

questions.description: In the small Cork-knit village of Ballyvogue, a local free parish… Advise David as to his legal position under the law…
questions.text: In the small Cork-knit village of Ballyvogue, a local free parish newsletter called "The Ballyvogue Bugle" is produced by residents of the village. The newsletter provides news, recipes and stories from within the village. The restaurant's staff felt anonymous sources from within the restaurant, a brand-new eatery writes the articles. The restaurant review published in the Bugle gave the restaurant terrible reviews. The newsletter did not name the author of the articles. David, the owner of Gillan's, does some research online and finds the restaurant review has been copied from the newsletter and posted on a restaurant review website where the article has since gone viral. David now seeks compensation for the damage caused to Gillan. Gillan argues she cannot be held responsible for something she did not write or post online. She further states that she cannot retract something that she is not responsible for. Advise David as to his legal position under the law of tort.

questions.description: Harry owned a semi-detached house in a large estate that he rented to… Advise Aoife as to any legal action that might…
questions.text: Harry owned a semi-detached house in a large estate that he rented to Alison. Almost immediately after Alison moved in, she installed a large heat pump that was extremely noisy. The noise was being generated to make a really loud noise during the winter months. Aoife complained to Alison many times about the noise but Alison thought Aoife was being overly sensitive and continued. As time went on, Aoife found she could not sleep at night. She also noticed that her pet dogs were becoming very distressed. Despite paying rent, Aoife went to the landlord Harry but he was not interested in causing her any problems, and he issued an order for eviction. Nothing happened as it was put on hold and stopped paying rent. Despite the order for eviction, Aoife remained in possession but now the noise of the heat pump was so bad, getting worse. Finally, Aoife could stand it no more and sought legal advice as to whether she could have Alison's heat pump permanently switched off. Advise Aoife as to any legal action that might be available to her under the law of tort.

questions.description: Critically discuss the calculation of damages in tort actions, in particular the assessment of future losses.
questions.text: Critically discuss the calculation of damages in tort actions, in particular the assessment of future losses.

questions.description: In a rugby match between a local secondary school, Liam, a player on the… Advise Liam and Lara as to their legal…
questions.text: In a rugby match between a local secondary school, Liam, a player on the home team, was tackled aggressively by Scott, a player on the away team. Scott's tackle was reckless and dangerous, causing Liam to suffer a serious knee injury. The referee did not penalise Scott for the tackle. Liam had to be taken off the field and subsequently underwent surgery. His recovery was expected to take 8-10 months. Scott claimed it was a legitimate tackle and he should not be held liable for injuries sustained during a contact sport. The school's sports teacher had not adequately explained the rules and safety procedures to Scott before the match. Lara, a spectator watching the match, was hit by a ball that went out of play. She suffered a broken nose. The school had not installed any safety netting or barriers around the pitch to protect spectators. Advise Liam and Lara as to their legal position under the law of tort.

questions.description: At a construction site for a new hospital, Luke was concluding a series of… Advise BIFF as to their legal position…
questions.text: At a construction site for a new hospital, Luke was concluding a series of power tests along various electrical lines. The main contractor, BIFF, had hired several workers including Luke to do work on an occupation basis. This testing is a standard safety procedure before the power would be switched on permanently. Luke was testing the power lines using electrical equipment, when another worker accidentally turned on one of the power switches. The power surged through the line that Luke was testing. The switch was poorly labeled and had a defective guard. On the explosion, it was found that Luke was walking to get to his work. He had to walk about 10 minutes to get to the site from the car park, along a path alongside the power lines. It would be easy to see that the path was always wet and slippery and was extremely dangerous. Bored with the waiting, one of the other workers on site picked up a nail gun and started firing nails at targets. Unfortunately, he hit Luke in the leg and ankle, seriously injuring him. Luke was not authorised to have a nail gun. BIFF are refusing any liability for the loss of power, saying Luke was not authorised to have a nail gun. Advise BIFF as to their legal position under the law of tort.

questions.description: In the world of luxury fashion, two high-end shoe brands, "Looboos" (an established firm over 100 years old) and…
questions.text: In the world of luxury fashion, two high-end shoe brands, "Looboos" (an established firm over 100 years old) and "Loofaus" (a new firm barely in existence for 10 years) have been competing for market share. Looboos, with its distinctive logo and elegant designs, has a significant reputation among consumers, although sales have flatlined as they are seen as a little dated. Recently, Loofaus launched a new shoe line, with a logo and packaging strikingly similar to Looboos and even using the tagline "Picking up where Looboos left off." Loofaus claim they are paying homage to Looboos' legacy. Looboos complains that Loofaus is trying to piggyback on their brand history and that they are deliberately setting themselves up to look as if they are an offshoot of Looboos. Loofaus claim the exact opposite, arguing that their whole approach is to create a feeling within the market that Looboos is an outdated brand that is being replaced by their modern, chic shoe line. They claim that people who wear their shoes would not be seen dead in Looboos. Critically discuss any potential action for Looboos in the law of tort.

questions.description: Critically discuss the law regarding fatal injuries in the law of tort paying particular attention to statutory…
questions.text: Critically discuss the law regarding fatal injuries in the law of tort paying particular attention to statutory authority and judicial precedent.
